GDPR_R14.title: Recital 14 GDPR
GDPR_R14.text: The protection afforded by this Regulation should apply to natural persons, whatever their nationality or place of residence, in relation to the processing of their personal data. This Regulation does not cover the processing of personal data which concerns legal persons and in particular undertakings established as legal persons, including the name and the form of the legal person and the contact details of the legal person.
GDPR_R14.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R14 — RECITAL — material scope: natural persons only
gdpr_protects(A) <= natural_person(A)

% GDPR_R14 — RECITAL — exclusion of legal-person data (positive 'not_personal_data' label)
not_personal_data(X) <= legal_person_data(X)

% GDPR_R14 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r14', 'protects_natural_persons_only')

GDPR_R15.title: Recital 15 GDPR
GDPR_R15.text: In order to prevent creating a serious risk of circumvention, the protection of natural persons should be technolo­ gically neutral and should not depend on the techniques used. The protection of natural persons should apply to the processing of personal data by automated means, as well as to manual processing, if the personal data are contained or are intended to be contained in a filing system. Files or sets of files, as well as their cover pages, which are not structured according to specific criteria should not fall within the scope of this Regulation.
GDPR_R15.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R15 — RECITAL — technological neutrality (drives material scope of A2.1)
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X) & automated_means(S)

% GDPR_R15 — RECITAL — manual processing within filing system also covered
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X) & filing_system(S)

% GDPR_R15 — RECITAL — unstructured files marked outside filing-system test
filing_system_excluded(S) <= unstructured_file_set(S)

% GDPR_R15 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r15', 'technologically_neutral')

GDPR_R16.title: Recital 16 GDPR
GDPR_R16.text: This Regulation does not apply to issues of protection of fundamental rights and freedoms or the free flow of personal data related to activities which fall outside the scope of Union law, such as activities concerning national security. This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data by the Member States when carrying out activities in relation to the common foreign and security policy of the Union.
GDPR_R16.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R16 — RECITAL — exclusion: outside Union law / national security
gdpr_excluded(S) <= activity_outside_union_law(S)

% GDPR_R16 — RECITAL — exclusion: CFSP (Title V Ch.2 TEU)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= cfsp_activity(S)

% GDPR_R16 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r16', 'no_application_outside_union_law')

GDPR_R17.title: Recital 17
GDPR_R17.text: Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council (2) applies to the processing of personal data by the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies. Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 and other Union legal acts applicable to such processing of personal data should be adapted to the principles and rules established in this Regulation and applied in the light of this Regulation. In order to provide a strong and coherent data protection framework in the Union, the necessary adaptations of Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 should follow after the adoption of this Regulation, in order to allow application at the same time as this Regulation.
GDPR_R17.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R17 — RECITAL — EU institutions covered by Reg 45/2001 (now 2018/1725)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= eu_institution_processing(S)

% GDPR_R17 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r17', 'eu_institutions_under_2018_1725')

GDPR_R18.title: Recital 18
GDPR_R18.text: This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity and thus with no connection to a professional or commercial activity. Personal or
GDPR_R18.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R18 — RECITAL — purely personal/household activity excluded
gdpr_excluded(S) <= purely_personal_household_activity(S)

% GDPR_R18 — RECITAL — narrative: still applies to controllers/processors providing means
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X) & provides_means_for_personal_processing(S)

% GDPR_R18 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r18', 'household_exception_narrow')

GDPR_R19.title: Recital 19
GDPR_R19.text:
The protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security and the free movement of such data, is the subject of a specific Union legal act. This Regulation should not, therefore, apply to processing activities for those purposes. However, personal data processed by public authorities under this Regulation should, when used for those purposes, be governed by a more specific Union legal act, namely Directive (EU) 2016/680 of the European Parliament and of the Council (1). Member States may entrust competent authorities within the meaning of Directive (EU) 2016/680 with tasks which are not necessarily carried out for the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and prevention of threats to public security, so that the processing of personal data for those other purposes, in so far as it is within the scope of Union law, falls within the scope of this Regulation.

With regard to the processing of personal data by those competent authorities for purposes falling within scope of this Regulation, Member States should be able to maintain or introduce more specific provisions to adapt the application of the rules of this Regulation. Such provisions may determine more precisely specific requirements for the processing of personal data by those competent authorities for those other purposes, taking into account the constitutional, organisational and administrative structure of the respective Member State. When the processing of personal data by private bodies falls within the scope of this Regulation, this Regulation should provide for the possibility for Member States under specific conditions to restrict by law certain obligations and rights when such a restriction constitutes a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard specific important interests including public security and the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security. This is relevant for instance in the framework of anti-money laundering or the activities of forensic laboratories.
GDPR_R19.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R19 — RECITAL — law-enforcement processing covered by Dir 2016/680
gdpr_excluded(S) <= led_law_enforcement_processing(S)

% GDPR_R19 — RECITAL — Member States may impose more specific rules within scope
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'r19', 'specific_provisions_for_competent_authorities')

% GDPR_R19 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r19', 'le_directive_lex_specialis')

GDPR_R20.title: Recital 20
GDPR_R20.text: While this Regulation applies, inter alia, to the activities of courts and other judicial authorities, Union or Member State law could specify the processing operations and processing procedures in relation to the processing of personal data by courts and other judicial authorities. The competence of the supervisory authorities should not cover the processing of personal data when courts are acting in their judicial capacity, in order to safeguard the independence of the judiciary in the performance of its judicial tasks, including decision- making. It should be possible to entrust supervision of such data processing operations to specific bodies within the judicial system of the Member State, which should, in particular ensure compliance with the rules of this Regulation, enhance awareness among members of the judiciary of their obligations under this Regulation and handle complaints in relation to such data processing operations.
GDPR_R20.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R20 — RECITAL — applies to courts/judicial authorities
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X) & by_court_or_judicial_authority(S)

% GDPR_R20 — RECITAL — supervisory authority does not cover judicial processing in judicial capacity
supervisory_competence_excluded(SA, S) <= supervisory_authority(SA) & court_in_judicial_capacity(C, S)

% GDPR_R20 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r20', 'judicial_independence_safeguard')

GDPR_R21.title: Recital 21
GDPR_R21.text: This Regulation is without prejudice to the application of Directive 2000/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (2), in particular of the liability rules of intermediary service providers in Articles 12 to 15 of that Directive. That Directive seeks to contribute to the proper functioning of the internal market by ensuring the free movement of information society services between Member States.
GDPR_R21.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R21 — RECITAL — without prejudice to e-Commerce Directive 2000/31/EC
intermediary_liability_safe_harbour(S) <= intermediary_service_provider(A) & processing(S) & by_actor(S, A)

% GDPR_R21 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r21', 'without_prejudice_to_2000_31_ec')

GDPR_R22.title: Recital 22
GDPR_R22.text:
Any processing of personal data in the context of the activities of an establishment of a controller or a processor in the Union should be carried out in accordance with this Regulation, regardless of whether the processing itself takes place within the Union. Establishment implies the effective and real exercise of activity through stable arrangements. The legal form of such arrangements, whether through a branch or a subsidiary with a legal personality, is not the determining factor in that respect.

(1) Directive (EU) 2016/680 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data by competent authorities for the purposes of prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, and the free movement of such data and repealing Council Framework Decision 2008/977/JHA (see page 89 of this Official Journal). (2) Directive 2000/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 8 June 2000 on certain legal aspects of information society services, in particular electronic commerce, in the Internal Market (‘Directive on electronic commerce’) (OJ L 178, 17.7.2000, p. 1).
GDPR_R22.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R22 — RECITAL — territorial: context-of-establishment limb
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & established_in(A, 'eu') & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A)

% GDPR_R22 — RECITAL — same for processors
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S) & established_in(A, 'eu') & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A)

% GDPR_R22 — RECITAL — establishment definition: stable arrangements regardless of legal form
establishment_real(A, J) <= effective_real_activity(A, J) & stable_arrangements(A, J)

% GDPR_R22 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r22', 'establishment_substance_over_form')

GDPR_R23.title: Recital 23
GDPR_R23.text: In order to ensure that natural persons are not deprived of the protection to which they are entitled under this Regulation, the processing of personal data of data subjects who are in the Union by a controller or a processor not established in the Union should be subject to this Regulation where the processing activities are related to offering goods or services to such data subjects irrespective of whether connected to a payment. In order to determine whether such a controller or processor is offering goods or services to data subjects who are in the Union, it should be ascertained whether it is apparent that the controller or processor envisages offering services to data subjects in one or more Member States in the Union. Whereas the mere accessibility of the controller's, processor's or an intermediary's website in the Union, of an email address or of other contact details, or the use of a language generally used in the third country where the controller is established, is insufficient to ascertain such intention, factors such as the use of a language or a currency generally used in one or more Member States with the possibility of ordering goods and services in that other language, or the mentioning of customers or users who are in the Union, may make it apparent that the controller envisages offering goods or services to data subjects in the Union.
GDPR_R23.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R23 — RECITAL — targeting limb of Art 3(2)(a)
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)

% GDPR_R23 — RECITAL — same for processors
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S) & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)

% GDPR_R23 — RECITAL — accessibility alone insufficient (positive flag)
data_subjects_in_eu_targeted_excluded(S, A) <= mere_website_accessibility(S, A) & ~targeting_indicia(S, A)

% GDPR_R23 — RECITAL — targeting indicia (language/currency/customer references)
targeting_indicia(S, A) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & use_of_eu_language(S)
targeting_indicia(S, A) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & use_of_eu_currency(S)
targeting_indicia(S, A) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & references_to_eu_customers(S)

% GDPR_R23 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r23', 'targeting_test_factors')

GDPR_R24.title: Recital 24
GDPR_R24.text: The processing of personal data of data subjects who are in the Union by a controller or processor not established in the Union should also be subject to this Regulation when it is related to the monitoring of the behaviour of such data subjects in so far as their behaviour takes place within the Union. In order to determine whether a processing activity can be considered to monitor the behaviour of data subjects, it should be ascertained whether natural persons are tracked on the internet including potential subsequent use of personal data processing techniques which consist of profiling a natural person, particularly in order to take decisions concerning her or him or for analysing or predicting her or his personal preferences, behaviours and attitudes.
GDPR_R24.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R24 — RECITAL — monitoring limb of Art 3(2)(b)
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S)

% GDPR_R24 — RECITAL — monitoring includes online tracking & profiling
monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S) <= online_tracking(A, S) & data_subjects_in_eu(S)
monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S) <= profiling_natural_person(A, S) & data_subjects_in_eu(S)

% GDPR_R24 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r24', 'monitoring_includes_profiling')

GDPR_R25.title: Recital 25
GDPR_R25.text: Where Member State law applies by virtue of public international law, this Regulation should also apply to a controller not established in the Union, such as in a Member State's diplomatic mission or consular post.
GDPR_R25.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R25 — RECITAL — Art 3(3) — MS law via public international law
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & member_state_law_via_intl_law(A)

% GDPR_R25 — RECITAL — example: diplomatic missions, consular posts
member_state_law_via_intl_law(A) <= diplomatic_mission(A)
member_state_law_via_intl_law(A) <= consular_post(A)

% GDPR_R25 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r25', 'public_intl_law_anchor')

GDPR_R26.title: Recital 26
GDPR_R26.text: The principles of data protection should apply to any information concerning an identified or identifiable natural person. Personal data which have undergone pseudonymisation, which could be attributed to a natural person by the use of additional information should be considered to be information on an identifiable natural person. To determine whether a natural person is identifiable, account should be taken of all the means reasonably likely to be used, such as singling out, either by the controller or by another person to identify the natural person directly or indirectly. To ascertain whether means are reasonably likely to be used to identify the natural person, account should be taken of all objective factors, such as the costs of and the amount of time required for identification, taking into consideration the available technology at the time of the processing and technological developments. The principles of data protection should therefore not apply to anonymous information, namely information which does not relate to an identified or identifiable natural person or to personal data rendered anonymous in such a manner that the data subject is not or no longer identifiable. This Regulation does not therefore concern the processing of such anonymous information, including for statistical or research purposes.
GDPR_R26.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R26 — RECITAL — pseudonymised data still personal data
personal_data(X) <= pseudonymised_data(X) & attributable_with_additional_info(X)

% GDPR_R26 — RECITAL — anonymous information is not personal data (positive label)
not_personal_data(X) <= anonymous_information(X)

% GDPR_R26 — RECITAL — identifiability test: means reasonably likely to be used
identifiable(X, A) <= concerns(X, A) & natural_person(A) & means_reasonably_likely(X, A)

% GDPR_R26 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r26', 'reasonably_likely_means_test')

GDPR_R27.title: Recital 27
GDPR_R27.text: This Regulation does not apply to the personal data of deceased persons. Member States may provide for rules regarding the processing of personal data of deceased persons.
GDPR_R27.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R27 — RECITAL — exclusion: deceased persons (positive label)
not_personal_data(X) <= deceased_personal_data(X)

% GDPR_R27 — RECITAL — Member States may regulate deceased-person data
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'r27', 'deceased_persons_data')

% GDPR_R27 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r27', 'deceased_data_excluded')

GDPR_R28.title: Recital 28
GDPR_R28.text: The application of pseudonymisation to personal data can reduce the risks to the data subjects concerned and help controllers and processors to meet their data-protection obligations. The explicit introduction of ‘pseudony­ misation’ in this Regulation is not intended to preclude any other measures of data protection.
GDPR_R28.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R28 — RECITAL — pseudonymisation reduces risk; not exclusive measure
risk_mitigated(S) <= pseudonymisation_applied(S)

% GDPR_R28 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r28', 'pseudonymisation_non_exclusive')

GDPR_R29.title: Recital 29
GDPR_R29.text: In order to create incentives to apply pseudonymisation when processing personal data, measures of pseudonymi­ sation should, whilst allowing general analysis, be possible within the same controller when that controller has taken technical and organisational measures necessary to ensure, for the processing concerned, that this Regulation is implemented, and that additional information for attributing the personal data to a specific data subject is kept separately. The controller processing the personal data should indicate the authorised persons within the same controller.
GDPR_R29.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R29 — RECITAL — pseudonymisation incentive: same controller may analyse
pseudonymisation_within_controller_allowed(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & pseudonymisation_applied(S) & technical_org_measures_in_place(A, S) & additional_info_kept_separately(A, S)

% GDPR_R29 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r29', 'pseudonymisation_incentive')

GDPR_R30.title: Recital 30
GDPR_R30.text: Natural persons may be associated with online identifiers provided by their devices, applications, tools and protocols, such as internet protocol addresses, cookie identifiers or other identifiers such as radio frequency identification tags. This may leave traces which, in particular when combined with unique identifiers and other information received by the servers, may be used to create profiles of the natural persons and identify them.
GDPR_R30.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R30 — RECITAL — online identifiers may identify natural persons
identifiable(X, A) <= online_identifier(X) & combined_with_unique_identifiers(X, A)
online_identifier_kinds('ip_address')
online_identifier_kinds('cookie_id')
online_identifier_kinds('rfid_tag')

% GDPR_R30 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r30', 'online_identifiers_within_personal_data')

GDPR_R31.title: Recital 31
GDPR_R31.text: Public authorities to which personal data are disclosed in accordance with a legal obligation for the exercise of their official mission, such as tax and customs authorities, financial investigation units, independent adminis­ trative authorities, or financial market authorities responsible for the regulation and supervision of securities markets should not be regarded as recipients if they receive personal data which are necessary to carry out a particular inquiry in the general interest, in accordance with Union or Member State law. The requests for disclosure sent by the public authorities should always be in writing, reasoned and occasional and should not concern the entirety of a filing system or lead to the interconnection of filing systems. The processing of personal data by those public authorities should comply with the applicable data-protection rules according to the purposes of the processing.
GDPR_R31.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R31 — RECITAL — public authorities receiving data in inquiry are not 'recipients' (positive label)
recipient_excluded(A, X) <= public_authority(A) & receives_in_general_interest_inquiry(A, X) & based_on_law(A)

% GDPR_R31 — RECITAL — disclosure requests: written, reasoned, occasional
disclosure_request_lawful(R) <= request_in_writing(R) & request_reasoned(R) & request_occasional(R) & ~concerns_entire_filing_system(R)

% GDPR_R31 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r31', 'inquiry_receipts_not_recipients')

GDPR_R171.title: Recital 171
GDPR_R171.text: Directive 95/46/EC should be repealed by this Regulation. Processing already under way on the date of application of this Regulation should be brought into conformity with this Regulation within the period of two years after which this Regulation enters into force. Where processing is based on consent pursuant to Directive 95/46/EC, it is not necessary for the data subject to give his or her consent again if the manner in which the consent has been given is in line with the conditions of this Regulation, so as to allow the controller to continue such processing after the date of application of this Regulation. Commission decisions adopted and authorisations by supervisory authorities based on Directive 95/46/EC remain in force until amended, replaced or repealed.
GDPR_R171.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_R171 — RECITAL — repeal of Directive 95/46/EC
+ transitional_grace('gdpr', 'directive_9546_repealed', '2018-05-25')

% GDPR_R171 — RECITAL — pre-existing consents preserved if conditions met
consent_carryover_valid(D, S) <= consent_under_dir_9546(D, S) & meets_gdpr_consent_conditions(D, S)

% GDPR_R171 — RECITAL — Commission decisions / SA authorisations remain in force
legacy_decision_in_force(X) <= decision_under_dir_9546(X) & ~amended(X) & ~repealed_decision(X)

% GDPR_R171 — RECITAL — narrative aid
recital_principle('gdpr', 'r171', 'transitional_continuity')

GDPR_A1.title: Subject-matter and objectives
GDPR_A1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_applies
applies(S, T, 'gdpr') <= in_force('gdpr', T)  & gdpr_territorial_link(A, S)  & gdpr_material(S)  & ~gdpr_excluded(S)

% GDPR_applies_with_actor
applies_via_actor(S, T, 'gdpr', A) <= in_force('gdpr', T)  & gdpr_territorial_link(A, S)  & gdpr_material(S)  & ~gdpr_excluded(S)

GDPR_A1.1.title: Article 1(1) GDPR
GDPR_A1.1.text: This Regulation lays down rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and rules relating to the free movement of personal data.
GDPR_A1.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A1_P1 — subject matter: rules for processing of personal data + free movement
gdpr_subject_matter(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X)
gdpr_subject_matter('free_movement_personal_data')

GDPR_A1.2.title: Article 1(2) GDPR
GDPR_A1.2.text: This Regulation protects fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons and in particular their right to the protection of personal data.
GDPR_A1.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A1_P2 — protects fundamental rights, in particular data protection
gdpr_protects_fundamental_right(A, 'data_protection') <= natural_person(A)

GDPR_A1.3.title: Article 1(3) GDPR
GDPR_A1.3.text: The free movement of personal data within the Union shall be neither restricted nor prohibited for reasons connected with the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data.
GDPR_A1.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A1_P3 — free movement: cannot be restricted on data-protection grounds (positive prohibition)
ms_must_not_restrict_free_movement_for_dp(MS, S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X) & member_state(MS)

GDPR_A2.title: Material scope
GDPR_A2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A2 — Material scope (chapeau; rules carried by paragraphs)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a2', 'material_scope')

GDPR_A2.1.text: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system.
GDPR_A2.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A2_P1_LIMB_1
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X)  & automated_means(x)

% GDPR_A2_P1_LIMB_2
gdpr_material(S) <= processing(S) & concerns(S, X) & personal_data(X)  & filing_system(S)

GDPR_A2.2.a.text: in the course of an activity which falls outside the scope of Union law;

GDPR_A2.2.b.text: by the Member States when carrying out activities which fall within the scope of Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU;
GDPR_A2.2.b.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A2_P2_PT_b — CFSP activities (Chapter 2 of Title V TEU)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= cfsp_activity(S)

GDPR_A2.2.c.text: by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity;

GDPR_A2.2.d.text: by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security.

GDPR_A2.2.text: This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data:
GDPR_A2.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A2_P2 — chapeau for the four exclusion limbs
gdpr_excluded(S) <= activity_outside_union_law(S)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= cfsp_activity(S)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= purely_personal_household_activity(S)
gdpr_excluded(S) <= led_law_enforcement_processing(S)

GDPR_A2.3.text: For the processing of personal data by the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies, Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 applies. Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 and other Union legal acts applicable to such processing of personal data shall be adapted to the principles and rules of this Regulation in accordance with Article 98.

GDPR_A2.4.text: This Regulation shall be without prejudice to the application of Directive 2000/31/EC, in particular of the liability rules of intermediary service providers in Articles 12 to 15 of that Directive.
GDPR_A2.4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A2_P4 — without prejudice to Directive 2000/31/EC (e-commerce)
intermediary_liability_safe_harbour(S) <= intermediary_service_provider(A) & by_actor(S, A) & processing(S)

GDPR_A3.title: Territorial scope
GDPR_A3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3 — Territorial scope (chapeau; rules carried by paragraphs)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a3', 'territorial_scope')

GDPR_A3.1.text: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data in the context of the activities of an establishment of a controller or a processor in the Union, regardless of whether the processing takes place in the Union or not.
GDPR_A3.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3_P1
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & established_in(A, 'eu')  & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A)

% GDPR_A3_P1
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S)  & established_in(A, 'eu')  & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A)

GDPR_A3.2.a.text: the offering of goods or services, irrespective of whether a payment of the data subject is required, to such data subjects in the Union; or
GDPR_A3.2.a.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3_P2_PT_a
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)

% GDPR_A3_P2_PT_a
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S)  & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)

GDPR_A3.2.b.text: the monitoring of their behaviour as far as their behaviour takes place within the Union.
GDPR_A3.2.b.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3_P2_PT_b
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S)

% GDPR_A3_P2_PT_b
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S)  & monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S)

GDPR_A3.2.text: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data of data subjects who are in the Union by a controller or processor not established in the Union, where the processing activities are related to:
GDPR_A3.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3_P2 — chapeau: extra-EU controllers/processors targeting EU subjects
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & ~established_in(A, 'eu') & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S) & ~established_in(A, 'eu') & data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, A)
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & ~established_in(A, 'eu') & monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S)
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= processor(A, S) & ~established_in(A, 'eu') & monitors_behaviour_in_eu(A, S)

GDPR_A3.3.text: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data by a controller not established in the Union, but in a place where Member State law applies by virtue of public international law.
GDPR_A3.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A3_P3
gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) <= controller(A, S) & member_state_law_via_intl_law(A)

GDPR_A4.title: Definitions
GDPR_A4.text: For the purposes of this Regulation:
GDPR_A4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P1_PT1
personal_data(X) <= concerns(X, A) & natural_person(A) & identifies(X, A)

GDPR_A4.1.text: ‘personal data’ means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person (‘data subject’); an identifiable natural person is one who can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier such as a name, an identification number, location data, an online identifier or to one or more factors specific to the physical, physiological, genetic, mental, economic, cultural or social identity of that natural person;
GDPR_A4.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P1 — 'personal data'
personal_data(X) <= concerns(X, A) & natural_person(A) & identifies(X, A)
personal_data(X) <= concerns(X, A) & natural_person(A) & identifiable(X, A)
identifies(X, A) <= contains_identifier(X, 'name', A)
identifies(X, A) <= contains_identifier(X, 'id_number', A)
identifies(X, A) <= contains_identifier(X, 'location_data', A)
identifies(X, A) <= contains_identifier(X, 'online_identifier', A)
identifiable(X, A) <= contains_factor(X, 'physical', A) | contains_factor(X, 'physiological', A) | contains_factor(X, 'genetic', A) | contains_factor(X, 'mental', A) | contains_factor(X, 'economic', A) | contains_factor(X, 'cultural', A) | contains_factor(X, 'social', A)

GDPR_A4.2.text: ‘processing’ means any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data or on sets of personal data, whether or not by automated means, such as collection, recording, organisation, structuring, storage, adaptation or alteration, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure by transmission, dissemination or otherwise making available, alignment or combination, restriction, erasure or destruction;
GDPR_A4.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P2 — 'processing'
processing(S) <= operation(S, X) & personal_data(X)
operation_kinds('collection')
operation_kinds('recording')
operation_kinds('organisation')
operation_kinds('structuring')
operation_kinds('storage')
operation_kinds('adaptation')
operation_kinds('alteration')
operation_kinds('retrieval')
operation_kinds('consultation')
operation_kinds('use')
operation_kinds('disclosure_by_transmission')
operation_kinds('dissemination')
operation_kinds('alignment')
operation_kinds('combination')
operation_kinds('restriction')
operation_kinds('erasure')
operation_kinds('destruction')

GDPR_A4.3.text: ‘restriction of processing’ means the marking of stored personal data with the aim of limiting their processing in the future;
GDPR_A4.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P3 — 'restriction of processing'
restriction_of_processing(S) <= processing(S) & marks_to_limit_future_processing(S)

GDPR_A4.4.text: ‘profiling’ means any form of automated processing of personal data consisting of the use of personal data to evaluate certain personal aspects relating to a natural person, in particular to analyse or predict aspects concerning that natural person's performance at work, economic situation, health, personal preferences, interests, reliability, behaviour, location or movements;
GDPR_A4.4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P4 — 'profiling'
profiling(S) <= processing(S) & automated_means(S) & evaluates_personal_aspects(S, A) & natural_person(A)

GDPR_A4.5.text: ‘pseudonymisation’ means the processing of personal data in such a manner that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without the use of additional information, provided that such additional information is kept separately and is subject to technical and organisational measures to ensure that the personal data are not attributed to an identified or identifiable natural person;
GDPR_A4.5.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P5 — 'pseudonymisation'
pseudonymisation_applied(S) <= processing(S) & data_no_longer_directly_attributable(S) & additional_info_kept_separately(_, S) & technical_org_measures_in_place(_, S)

GDPR_A4.6.text: ‘filing system’ means any structured set of personal data which are accessible according to specific criteria, whether centralised, decentralised or dispersed on a functional or geographical basis;

GDPR_A4.7.text: ‘controller’ means the natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data; where the purposes and means of such processing are determined by Union or Member State law, the controller or the specific criteria for its nomination may be provided for by Union or Member State law;
GDPR_A4.7.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P7 — 'controller'
controller(A, S) <= determines_purposes_and_means(A, S) & processing(S)
controller(A, S) <= designated_by_law_as_controller(A, S)

GDPR_A4.8.text: ‘processor’ means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which processes personal data on behalf of the controller;
GDPR_A4.8.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P8 — 'processor'
processor(A, S) <= processes_on_behalf_of_controller(A, S, _) & processing(S)

GDPR_A4.9.text:
‘recipient’ means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or another body, to which the personal data are disclosed, whether a third party or not. However, public authorities which may receive personal data in the

framework of a particular inquiry in accordance with Union or Member State law shall not be regarded as recipients; the processing of those data by those public authorities shall be in compliance with the applicable data protection rules according to the purposes of the processing;
GDPR_A4.9.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P9 — 'recipient'
recipient(A, X) <= disclosed_to(X, A) & ~recipient_excluded(A, X)
public_authority_inquiry_recipient(A, X) <= public_authority(A) & receives_in_general_interest_inquiry(A, X)
recipient_excluded(A, X) <= public_authority_inquiry_recipient(A, X)

GDPR_A4.10.text: ‘third party’ means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or body other than the data subject, controller, processor and persons who, under the direct authority of the controller or processor, are authorised to process personal data;
GDPR_A4.10.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P10 — 'third party'
third_party(A, S) <= actor(A) & processing(S) & ~data_subject_of(A, S) & ~controller(A, S) & ~processor(A, S) & ~under_direct_authority(A, S)

GDPR_A4.11.text: ‘consent’ of the data subject means any freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of the data subject's wishes by which he or she, by a statement or by a clear affirmative action, signifies agreement to the processing of personal data relating to him or her;
GDPR_A4.11.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P11 — 'consent'
consent_given(D, S, P) <= freely_given(D, S) & specific(D, S, P) & informed(D, S) & unambiguous_indication(D, S) & clear_affirmative_action(D, S)

GDPR_A4.12.text: ‘personal data breach’ means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed;
GDPR_A4.12.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P12 — 'personal data breach'
personal_data_breach(B) <= security_breach(B) & affects_personal_data(B) & breach_kind(B, 'destruction')
personal_data_breach(B) <= security_breach(B) & affects_personal_data(B) & breach_kind(B, 'loss')
personal_data_breach(B) <= security_breach(B) & affects_personal_data(B) & breach_kind(B, 'alteration')
personal_data_breach(B) <= security_breach(B) & affects_personal_data(B) & breach_kind(B, 'unauthorised_disclosure')
personal_data_breach(B) <= security_breach(B) & affects_personal_data(B) & breach_kind(B, 'unauthorised_access')

GDPR_A4.13.text: ‘genetic data’ means personal data relating to the inherited or acquired genetic characteristics of a natural person which give unique information about the physiology or the health of that natural person and which result, in particular, from an analysis of a biological sample from the natural person in question;
GDPR_A4.13.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P13 — 'genetic data'
genetic_data(X) <= personal_data(X) & relates_to_genetic_characteristics(X)
category(X, 'genetic') <= genetic_data(X)

GDPR_A4.14.text: ‘biometric data’ means personal data resulting from specific technical processing relating to the physical, physio­ logical or behavioural characteristics of a natural person, which allow or confirm the unique identification of that natural person, such as facial images or dactyloscopic data;
GDPR_A4.14.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P14 — 'biometric data'
biometric_data(X) <= personal_data(X) & from_specific_technical_processing(X) & relates_to_physical_or_behavioural(X)
category(X, 'biometric_for_id') <= biometric_data(X) & for_unique_identification(X)

GDPR_A4.15.text: ‘data concerning health’ means personal data related to the physical or mental health of a natural person, including the provision of health care services, which reveal information about his or her health status;
GDPR_A4.15.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P15 — 'data concerning health'
health_data(X) <= personal_data(X) & relates_to_health_status(X)
category(X, 'health') <= health_data(X)

GDPR_A4.16.a.text: as regards a controller with establishments in more than one Member State, the place of its central adminis­ tration in the Union, unless the decisions on the purposes and means of the processing of personal data are taken in another establishment of the controller in the Union and the latter establishment has the power to have such decisions implemented, in which case the establishment having taken such decisions is to be considered to be the main establishment;
GDPR_A4.16.a.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P16_PT_a — controller's main establishment
main_establishment(A, J) <= controller(A, _) & multiple_eu_establishments(A) & central_administration_in(A, J) & ~main_decisions_taken_elsewhere(A)
main_establishment(A, J) <= controller(A, _) & multiple_eu_establishments(A) & main_decisions_taken_at(A, J) & implementation_power_at(A, J)

GDPR_A4.16.b.text: as regards a processor with establishments in more than one Member State, the place of its central adminis­ tration in the Union, or, if the processor has no central administration in the Union, the establishment of the processor in the Union where the main processing activities in the context of the activities of an establishment of the processor take place to the extent that the processor is subject to specific obligations under this Regulation;
GDPR_A4.16.b.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P16_PT_b — processor's main establishment
main_establishment(A, J) <= processor(A, _) & multiple_eu_establishments(A) & central_administration_in(A, J)
main_establishment(A, J) <= processor(A, _) & ~central_administration_in(A, _) & main_processing_activities_at(A, J)

GDPR_A4.16.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P16 — 'main establishment' chapeau (rules carried by points)
main_establishment(A, J) <= controller(A, _) & central_administration_in(A, J) & ~main_decisions_taken_elsewhere(A)
main_establishment(A, J) <= processor(A, _) & central_administration_in(A, J)

GDPR_A4.17.text: ‘representative’ means a natural or legal person established in the Union who, designated by the controller or processor in writing pursuant to Article 27, represents the controller or processor with regard to their respective obligations under this Regulation;
GDPR_A4.17.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P17 — 'representative'
representative(R, A) <= established_in(R, 'eu') & designated_by(A, R) & represents_for_compliance(R, A)

GDPR_A4.18.text: ‘enterprise’ means a natural or legal person engaged in an economic activity, irrespective of its legal form, including partnerships or associations regularly engaged in an economic activity;

GDPR_A4.19.text: ‘group of undertakings’ means a controlling undertaking and its controlled undertakings;
GDPR_A4.19.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P19 — 'group of undertakings'
group_of_undertakings(G) <= controlling_undertaking(G, U_top) & controlled_undertaking(G, U_sub)

GDPR_A4.20.text: ‘binding corporate rules’ means personal data protection policies which are adhered to by a controller or processor established on the territory of a Member State for transfers or a set of transfers of personal data to a controller or processor in one or more third countries within a group of undertakings, or group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity;
GDPR_A4.20.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P20 — 'binding corporate rules'
bcr(P, G) <= data_protection_policy(P) & adhered_by_eu_controller_or_processor(P, A) & for_intra_group_transfers(P, G)

GDPR_A4.21.text: ‘supervisory authority’ means an independent public authority which is established by a Member State pursuant to Article 51;

GDPR_A4.22.a_2.text: the controller or processor is established on the territory of the Member State of that supervisory authority;
GDPR_A4.22.a_2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P22_PT_a — controller/processor established in MS of SA
sa_concerned_basis(SA, A, S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & established_in_ms_of(A, SA)

GDPR_A4.22.b_2.text: data subjects residing in the Member State of that supervisory authority are substantially affected or likely to be substantially affected by the processing; or
GDPR_A4.22.b_2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P22_PT_b — data subjects in MS of SA substantially affected
sa_concerned_basis(SA, A, S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & data_subjects_in_ms_of(SA) & substantially_affected(S, SA)

GDPR_A4.22.c_2.text: a complaint has been lodged with that supervisory authority;
GDPR_A4.22.c_2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P22_PT_c — complaint lodged with SA
sa_concerned_basis(SA, A, S) <= complaint_lodged_with(SA, S) & controller_or_processor(A, S)

GDPR_A4.22.text: ‘supervisory authority concerned’ means a supervisory authority which is concerned by the processing of personal data because:
GDPR_A4.22.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P22 — 'supervisory authority concerned' chapeau
supervisory_authority_concerned(SA, A, S) <= supervisory_authority(SA) & sa_concerned_basis(SA, A, S)

GDPR_A4.23.a_3.text: processing of personal data which takes place in the context of the activities of establishments in more than one Member State of a controller or processor in the Union where the controller or processor is established in more than one Member State; or
GDPR_A4.23.a_3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P23_PT_a — multiple-MS establishments
cross_border_basis_a(S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & multiple_eu_establishments(A) & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A)

GDPR_A4.23.b_3.text: processing of personal data which takes place in the context of the activities of a single establishment of a controller or processor in the Union but which substantially affects or is likely to substantially affect data subjects in more than one Member State.
GDPR_A4.23.b_3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P23_PT_b — single establishment but cross-MS impact
cross_border_basis_b(S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & processing_in_context_of_establishment(S, A) & substantially_affects_subjects_in_multiple_ms(S)

GDPR_A4.23.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P23 — 'cross-border processing' chapeau
cross_border_processing(S) <= cross_border_basis_a(S)
cross_border_processing(S) <= cross_border_basis_b(S)

GDPR_A4.24.text: ‘relevant and reasoned objection’ means an objection to a draft decision as to whether there is an infringement of this Regulation, or whether envisaged action in relation to the controller or processor complies with this Regulation, which clearly demonstrates the significance of the risks posed by the draft decision as regards the fundamental rights and freedoms of data subjects and, where applicable, the free flow of personal data within the Union;
GDPR_A4.24.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P24 — 'relevant and reasoned objection'
relevant_reasoned_objection(O) <= objection_to_draft_decision(O) & demonstrates_significance_of_risks(O)

GDPR_A4.25.text: ‘information society service’ means a service as defined in point (b) of Article 1(1) of Directive (EU) 2015/1535 of the European Parliament and of the Council (1);

GDPR_A4.26.text:
‘international organisation’ means an organisation and its subordinate bodies governed by public international law, or any other body which is set up by, or on the basis of, an agreement between two or more countries.

CHAPTER II

Principles
GDPR_A4.26.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A4_P26 — 'international organisation'
international_organisation(O) <= governed_by_public_intl_law(O)
international_organisation(O) <= setup_by_intercountry_agreement(O)

GDPR_A9.title: Processing of special categories of personal data

GDPR_A9.1.text: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
GDPR_A9.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P1
special_category_data(X) <= category(X, 'racial') | category(X, 'ethnic')  | category(X, 'political_opinions') | category(X, 'religious')  | category(X, 'philosophical') | category(X, 'trade_union')  | category(X, 'genetic') | category(X, 'biometric_for_id')  | category(X, 'health') | category(X, 'sex_life') | category(X, 'sexual_orientation')

GDPR_A9.2.a.text: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;
GDPR_A9.2.a.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_a — explicit consent
art9_lawful(S) <= explicit_consent_given(D, S, _) & ~ms_law_prohibits_consent_lift(S)

GDPR_A9.2.b.text: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rights of the controller or of the data subject in the field of employment and social security and social protection law in so far as it is authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
GDPR_A9.2.b.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_b — employment / social security obligations
art9_lawful(S) <= necessary_for_employment_social_security(S) & authorised_by_law_or_collective_agreement(S) & appropriate_safeguards_present(S)

GDPR_A9.2.c.text: processing is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject or of another natural person where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent;
GDPR_A9.2.c.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_c — vital interests where subject incapable
art9_lawful(S) <= necessary_to_protect_vital_interests(S) & data_subject_incapable_of_consent(S)

GDPR_A9.2.d.text: processing is carried out in the course of its legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards by a foundation, association or any other not-for-profit body with a political, philosophical, religious or trade union aim and on condition that the processing relates solely to the members or to former members of the body or to persons who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;
GDPR_A9.2.d.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_d — not-for-profit body legitimate activities
art9_lawful(S) <= legitimate_activity_of_nonprofit(S) & relates_to_members_or_close(S) & ~discloses_outside_without_consent(S) & appropriate_safeguards_present(S)

GDPR_A9.2.e.text: processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
GDPR_A9.2.e.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_e — manifestly made public by data subject
art9_lawful(S) <= manifestly_made_public_by_subject(S)

GDPR_A9.2.f.text: processing is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or whenever courts are acting in their judicial capacity;
GDPR_A9.2.f.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_f — legal claims / judicial capacity
art9_lawful(S) <= necessary_for_legal_claims(S)
art9_lawful(S) <= courts_acting_in_judicial_capacity(S)

GDPR_A9.2.g.text: processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest, on the basis of Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;
GDPR_A9.2.g.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_g — substantial public interest, EU/MS law basis
art9_lawful(S) <= substantial_public_interest_basis(S) & proportionate_to_aim(S) & specific_safeguards(S)

GDPR_A9.2.h.i.text: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protecting against serious cross-border threats to health or ensuring high standards of quality and safety of health care and of medicinal products or medical devices, on the basis of Union or Member State law which provides for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject, in particular professional secrecy;
GDPR_A9.2.h.i.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_h_i — public health (cross-border threats, quality/safety)
art9_lawful(S) <= necessary_for_public_health(S) & eu_or_ms_law_basis(S) & specific_safeguards(S)

GDPR_A9.2.h.text: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3;
GDPR_A9.2.h.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_h — preventive/occupational medicine, health/social care
art9_lawful(S) <= necessary_for_health_or_social_care(S) & basis_in_eu_or_ms_law_or_contract(S) & art9_p3_secrecy_satisfied(S)

GDPR_A9.2.j.text: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be propor­ tionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject.
GDPR_A9.2.j.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2_PT_j — archiving / scientific research / statistics (Art 89(1))
art9_lawful(S) <= for_archiving_research_statistics(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & proportionate_to_aim(S)

GDPR_A9.2.text: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies:
GDPR_A9.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P2 — derogations chapeau (positive 'lifted' label)
art9_prohibition_lifted(S) <= art9_lawful(S)

GDPR_A9.3.text: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.
GDPR_A9.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P3 — professional secrecy condition for point (h)
art9_p3_secrecy_satisfied(S) <= processed_under_secrecy_obligation(S)

GDPR_A9.4.text: Member States may maintain or introduce further conditions, including limitations, with regard to the processing of genetic data, biometric data or data concerning health.
GDPR_A9.4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A9_P4 — Member-State derogation: stricter conditions on genetic/biometric/health
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a9_p4', 'genetic_biometric_health_stricter')

GDPR_A10.title: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences
GDPR_A10.text: Processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences or related security measures based on Article 6(1) shall be carried out only under the control of official authority or when the processing is authorised by Union or Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of data subjects. Any comprehensive register of criminal convictions shall be kept only under the control of official authority.

GDPR_A11.title: Processing which does not require identification
GDPR_A11.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A11 — chapeau (rules carried by paragraphs)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a11', 'processing_without_identification')

GDPR_A11.1.text: If the purposes for which a controller processes personal data do not or do no longer require the identification of a data subject by the controller, the controller shall not be obliged to maintain, acquire or process additional information in order to identify the data subject for the sole purpose of complying with this Regulation.
GDPR_A11.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A11_P1 — controller need not maintain identification info merely for compliance
obligation_disapplied(A, S, 'maintain_identification_info_for_compliance') <= controller(A, S) & processing(S) & ~purpose_requires_identification(S)

GDPR_A11.2.text:
Where, in cases referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, the controller is able to demonstrate that it is not in a position to identify the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject accordingly, if possible. In such cases, Articles 15 to 20 shall not apply except where the data subject, for the purpose of exercising his or her rights under those articles, provides additional information enabling his or her identification.

CHAPTER III

Rights of the data subject

Sec ti on 1

Tr an s p ar enc y a nd mo da li tie s
GDPR_A11.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A11_P2 — Articles 15-20 disapplied where controller cannot identify
right_disapplied(D, S, 'access') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & ~subject_provided_additional_info(D, S)
right_disapplied(D, S, 'rectification') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & ~subject_provided_additional_info(D, S)
right_disapplied(D, S, 'erasure') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & ~subject_provided_additional_info(D, S)
right_disapplied(D, S, 'restriction') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & ~subject_provided_additional_info(D, S)
right_disapplied(D, S, 'portability') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & ~subject_provided_additional_info(D, S)

obligation(A, S, 'inform_subject_of_inability_to_identify') <= controller(A, S) & cannot_identify_subject(A, S, D) & possible_to_inform(A, S)

GDPR_A23.1.d.text: the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security;
GDPR_A23.1.d.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P1_PT_d — criminal offences / penalties / public-security threats
restriction_objective_lawful('criminal_offences_prevention_investigation_prosecution')

GDPR_A23.1.e.text: other important objectives of general public interest of the Union or of a Member State, in particular an important economic or financial interest of the Union or of a Member State, including monetary, budgetary and taxation a matters, public health and social security;
GDPR_A23.1.e.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P1_PT_e — important objectives of general public interest
restriction_objective_lawful('general_public_interest_economic_financial')

GDPR_A23.1.f.text: the protection of judicial independence and judicial proceedings;

GDPR_A23.1.g.text: the prevention, investigation, detection and prosecution of breaches of ethics for regulated professions;
GDPR_A23.1.g.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P1_PT_g — breaches of ethics for regulated professions
restriction_objective_lawful('regulated_profession_ethics')

GDPR_A23.1.h.i.text: the protection of the data subject or the rights and freedoms of others;
GDPR_A23.1.h.i.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P1_PT_h_i — protection of data subject and rights/freedoms of others
restriction_objective_lawful('protection_of_subject_or_others')

GDPR_A23.1.h.text: a monitoring, inspection or regulatory function connected, even occasionally, to the exercise of official authority in the cases referred to in points (a) to (e) and (g);

GDPR_A23.1.j.text: the enforcement of civil law claims.

GDPR_A23.1.text: Union or Member State law to which the data controller or processor is subject may restrict by way of a legislative measure the scope of the obligations and rights provided for in Articles 12 to 22 and Article 34, as well as Article 5 in so far as its provisions correspond to the rights and obligations provided for in Articles 12 to 22, when such a restriction respects the essence of the fundamental rights and freedoms and is a necessary and proportionate measure in a democratic society to safeguard:
GDPR_A23.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P1 — MS law may restrict A12-22, A34, A5 (mirror)
ms_restriction_basis(MS, S, R) <= ms_law_restricts(MS, S, R) & respects_essence_of_rights(MS, S) & necessary_proportionate_in_democratic_society(MS, S) & restriction_safeguards_objective(MS, R)

GDPR_A23.2.a.text: the purposes of the processing or categories of processing;

GDPR_A23.2.c.text: the scope of the restrictions introduced;

GDPR_A23.2.d.text: the safeguards to prevent abuse or unlawful access or transfer;

GDPR_A23.2.e.text: the specification of the controller or categories of controllers;

GDPR_A23.2.f.text: the storage periods and the applicable safeguards taking into account the nature, scope and purposes of the processing or categories of processing;

GDPR_A23.2.g.text: the risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects; and

GDPR_A23.2.h.text:
the right of data subjects to be informed about the restriction, unless that may be prejudicial to the purpose of the restriction.

CHAPTER IV

Controller and processor

Sec ti on 1

G e ner a l o bl iga tio ns
GDPR_A23.2.h.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P2_PT_h — right of subject to be informed of restriction
restriction_specification_kind('subject_right_to_be_informed')

GDPR_A23.2.text: In particular, any legislative measure referred to in paragraph 1 shall contain specific provisions at least, where relevant, as to:
GDPR_A23.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A23_P2 — required content of restriction legislation (chapeau)
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'purposes')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'data_categories')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'scope')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'safeguards')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'controller_specification')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'storage_periods')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'risks_to_rights')
ms_restriction_must_specify(MS, R, 'right_to_be_informed')

GDPR_A27.title: Representatives of controllers or processors not established in the Union
GDPR_A27.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27 — Representatives of non-EU controllers/processors (chapeau)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a27', 'non_eu_representative')

GDPR_A27.1.text: Where Article 3(2) applies, the controller or the processor shall designate in writing a representative in the Union.
GDPR_A27.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P1 — designate representative when Art 3(2) applies
obligation(A, S, 'designate_eu_representative') <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & ~established_in(A, 'eu') & gdpr_territorial_link(A, S) & art3_p2_applies(S)
art3_p2_applies(S) <= data_subjects_in_eu_targeted(S, _)
art3_p2_applies(S) <= monitors_behaviour_in_eu(_, S)

GDPR_A27.2.a.text: processing which is occasional, does not include, on a large scale, processing of special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or processing of personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10, and is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, taking into account the nature, context, scope and purposes of the processing; or
GDPR_A27.2.a.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P2_PT_a — occasional, low-risk processing exemption
a27_p2_exempt(A, S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & processing_occasional(S) & ~large_scale_special_data(S) & ~large_scale_criminal_data(S) & ~likely_to_risk_rights(S)

GDPR_A27.2.b.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P2_PT_b — public authority/body exemption
a27_p2_exempt(A, S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & public_authority(A)
a27_p2_exempt(A, S) <= controller_or_processor(A, S) & public_body(A)

GDPR_A27.2.text: The obligation laid down in paragraph 1 of this Article shall not apply to:
GDPR_A27.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P2 — exemption chapeau
obligation_disapplied(A, S, 'designate_eu_representative') <= a27_p2_exempt(A, S)

GDPR_A27.3.text: The representative shall be established in one of the Member States where the data subjects, whose personal data are processed in relation to the offering of goods or services to them, or whose behaviour is monitored, are.
GDPR_A27.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P3 — representative location: in MS where targeting/monitoring occurs
obligation(A, S, 'representative_in_relevant_ms') <= obligation(A, S, 'designate_eu_representative') & representative(R, A) & established_in_ms_of_targeting(R, S)

GDPR_A27.4.text: The representative shall be mandated by the controller or processor to be addressed in addition to or instead of the controller or the processor by, in particular, supervisory authorities and data subjects, on all issues related to processing, for the purposes of ensuring compliance with this Regulation.
GDPR_A27.4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P4 — representative mandate
obligation(A, R, 'mandate_representative_for_compliance') <= controller_or_processor(A, _) & representative(R, A)
addressable_for_compliance(R, A) <= representative(R, A) & mandated_by(R, A)

GDPR_A27.5.text: The designation of a representative by the controller or processor shall be without prejudice to legal actions which could be initiated against the controller or the processor themselves.
GDPR_A27.5.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A27_P5 — designation does not preclude legal action against principal (positive label)
non_exclusive_liability_of_representative(R, A) <= representative(R, A) & controller_or_processor(A, S)

GDPR_A85.title: Processing and freedom of expression and information
GDPR_A85.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A85 — Processing & freedom of expression (chapeau)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a85', 'reconcile_with_freedom_of_expression')

GDPR_A85.1.text: Member States shall by law reconcile the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation with the right to freedom of expression and information, including processing for journalistic purposes and the purposes of academic, artistic or literary expression.
GDPR_A85.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A85_P1 — MS shall reconcile DP with freedom of expression
obligation(MS, _, 'reconcile_dp_with_expression') <= member_state(MS)

GDPR_A85.2.text: For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary expression, Member States shall provide for exemptions or derogations from Chapter II (principles), Chapter III (rights of the data subject), Chapter IV (controller and processor), Chapter V (transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations), Chapter VI (independent supervisory authorities), Chapter VII (cooperation and consistency) and Chapter IX (specific data processing situations) if they are necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information.
GDPR_A85.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A85_P2 — MS provide exemptions for journalism/academic/artistic/literary
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a85_p2', 'journalism_academic_artistic_literary_expression')
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_ii') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_iii') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_iv') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_v') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_vi') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_vii') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)
ms_derogation_chapter(MS, 'gdpr', 'chapter_ix') <= ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)

GDPR_A85.3.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it has adopted pursuant to paragraph 2 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.
GDPR_A85.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A85_P3 — MS shall notify Commission of A85(2) provisions
obligation(MS, _, 'notify_commission_of_a85_p2_provisions') <= member_state(MS) & ms_provides_a85_p2_exemption(MS)

GDPR_A86.title: Processing and public access to official documents
GDPR_A86.text: Personal data in official documents held by a public authority or a public body or a private body for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest may be disclosed by the authority or body in accordance with Union or Member State law to which the public authority or body is subject in order to reconcile public access to official documents with the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation.
GDPR_A86.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A86 — Public access to official documents
disclosure_lawful(A, X) <= public_authority_or_body(A) & official_document(X) & disclosure_per_eu_or_ms_law(A, X)

GDPR_A87.title: Processing of the national identification number
GDPR_A87.text: Member States may further determine the specific conditions for the processing of a national identification number or any other identifier of general application. In that case the national identification number or any other identifier of general application shall be used only under appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of the data subject pursuant to this Regulation.
GDPR_A87.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A87 — National identification number
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a87', 'national_identification_number_conditions')
obligation(A, S, 'use_national_id_with_safeguards') <= uses_national_id_or_general_identifier(A, S)

GDPR_A88.title: Processing in the context of employment

GDPR_A88.1.text: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees' personal data in the employment context, in particular for the purposes of the recruitment, the performance of the contract of employment, including discharge of obligations laid down by law or by collective agreements, management, planning and organisation of work, equality and diversity in the workplace, health and safety at work, protection of employer's or customer's property and for the purposes of the exercise and enjoyment, on an individual or collective basis, of rights and benefits related to employment, and for the purpose of the termination of the employment relationship.
GDPR_A88.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A88_P1 — MS may provide more specific rules for employment processing
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a88_p1', 'employment_context_specific_rules')

GDPR_A88.2.text: Those rules shall include suitable and specific measures to safeguard the data subject's human dignity, legitimate interests and fundamental rights, with particular regard to the transparency of processing, the transfer of personal data within a group of undertakings, or a group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity and monitoring systems at the work place.
GDPR_A88.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A88_P2 — those rules must include suitable safeguards
ms_a88_rules_required(MS, 'transparency')
ms_a88_rules_required(MS, 'intra_group_transfers')
ms_a88_rules_required(MS, 'workplace_monitoring_systems')
ms_a88_rules_required(MS, 'human_dignity_safeguards')

GDPR_A88.3.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission those provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
GDPR_A88.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A88_P3 — MS shall notify Commission by 25 May 2018
obligation(MS, _, 'notify_commission_of_a88_p1_provisions') <= member_state(MS) & ms_provides_a88_p1_rules(MS)
+ notification_deadline('gdpr', 'a88_p1', '2018-05-25')

GDPR_A89.text: Safeguards and derogations relating to processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes

GDPR_A89.1.text:
Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject. Those safeguards shall ensure that technical and organisational measures are in place in particular in

order to ensure respect for the principle of data minimisation. Those measures may include pseudonymisation provided that those purposes can be fulfilled in that manner. Where those purposes can be fulfilled by further processing which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.
GDPR_A89.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A89_P1 — appropriate safeguards required
art89_safeguards_present(S) <= for_archiving_research_statistics(S) & technical_org_measures_in_place(_, S) & data_minimisation_respected(S)
art89_safeguards_present(S) <= for_archiving_research_statistics(S) & pseudonymisation_applied(S)
obligation(A, S, 'fulfil_purposes_without_identification_if_possible') <= controller(A, S) & for_archiving_research_statistics(S) & purposes_fulfillable_without_identification(S)

GDPR_A89.2.text: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.
GDPR_A89.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A89_P2 — derogations for research/statistics from rights 15,16,18,21
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a89_p2', 'research_statistics_rights_derogation')
right_disapplied(D, S, 'access') <= data_subject_of(D, S) & for_research_or_statistics(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & ms_derogates_a89_p2(S, 'access') & rights_render_purpose_impossible(S, 'access')
right_disapplied(D, S, 'rectification') <= data_subject_of(D, S) & for_research_or_statistics(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & ms_derogates_a89_p2(S, 'rectification') & rights_render_purpose_impossible(S, 'rectification')
right_disapplied(D, S, 'restriction') <= data_subject_of(D, S) & for_research_or_statistics(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & ms_derogates_a89_p2(S, 'restriction') & rights_render_purpose_impossible(S, 'restriction')
right_disapplied(D, S, 'object') <= data_subject_of(D, S) & for_research_or_statistics(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & ms_derogates_a89_p2(S, 'object') & rights_render_purpose_impossible(S, 'object')

GDPR_A89.3.text: Where personal data are processed for archiving purposes in the public interest, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18, 19, 20 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.
GDPR_A89.3.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A89_P3 — derogations for archiving from rights 15,16,18,19,20,21
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a89_p3', 'archiving_rights_derogation')
right_disapplied(D, S, R) <= data_subject_of(D, S) & for_archiving_public_interest(S) & art89_safeguards_present(S) & ms_derogates_a89_p3(S, R) & rights_render_purpose_impossible(S, R) & archiving_derogated_right(R)
archiving_derogated_right('access')
archiving_derogated_right('rectification')
archiving_derogated_right('restriction')
archiving_derogated_right('notification_of_recipients')
archiving_derogated_right('portability')
archiving_derogated_right('object')

GDPR_A89.4.text: Where processing referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 serves at the same time another purpose, the derogations shall apply only to processing for the purposes referred to in those paragraphs.
GDPR_A89.4.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A89_P4 — derogations apply only to the relevant purposes when joint
derogation_blocked(S, R) <= dual_purpose(S, P_research, P_other) & archiving_derogated_right(R) & ~for_purpose(R, P_research)

GDPR_A90.1.text: Member States may adopt specific rules to set out the powers of the supervisory authorities laid down in points (e) and (f) of Article 58(1) in relation to controllers or processors that are subject, under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies, to an obligation of professional secrecy or other equivalent obligations of secrecy where this is necessary and proportionate to reconcile the right of the protection of personal data with the obligation of secrecy. Those rules shall apply only with regard to personal data which the controller or processor has received as a result of or has obtained in an activity covered by that obligation of secrecy.
GDPR_A90.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A90_P1 — MS may set out powers of SA in relation to secrecy-bound C/P
ms_derogation_basis('gdpr', 'a90_p1', 'sa_powers_vs_professional_secrecy')

GDPR_A90.2.text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the rules adopted pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.
GDPR_A90.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A90_P2 — MS shall notify Commission by 25 May 2018
obligation(MS, _, 'notify_commission_of_a90_p1_provisions') <= member_state(MS) & ms_provides_a90_p1_rules(MS)
+ notification_deadline('gdpr', 'a90_p1', '2018-05-25')

GDPR_A91.title: Existing data protection rules of churches and religious associations
GDPR_A91.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A91 — Churches and religious associations (chapeau)
gdpr_scope_article('gdpr_a91', 'churches_religious_associations')

GDPR_A91.1.text: Where in a Member State, churches and religious associations or communities apply, at the time of entry into force of this Regulation, comprehensive rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to processing, such rules may continue to apply, provided that they are brought into line with this Regulation.
GDPR_A91.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A91_P1 — pre-existing comprehensive rules may continue if aligned
church_rules_carryover_valid(MS, RC) <= church_or_religious_association(RC) & comprehensive_rules_at_entry_into_force(RC, MS) & brought_in_line_with_gdpr(RC)

GDPR_A91.2.text:
Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of this Article shall be subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority, which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation.

CHAPTER X

Delegated acts and implementing acts
GDPR_A91.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A91_P2 — those associations subject to independent SA supervision
obligation(RC, _, 'subject_to_independent_sa_supervision') <= church_rules_carryover_valid(_, RC)

GDPR_A94.1.text: Directive 95/46/EC is repealed with effect from 25 May 2018.
GDPR_A94.1.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A94_P1 — repeal of Directive 95/46/EC effective 2018-05-25
+ directive_repealed('directive_9546', '2018-05-25')

GDPR_A94.2.text: References to the repealed Directive shall be construed as references to this Regulation. References to the Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data established by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC shall be construed as references to the European Data Protection Board established by this Regulation.
GDPR_A94.2.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A94_P2 — references to Dir 95/46/EC construed as references to GDPR
+ reference_construction('directive_9546', 'gdpr')
+ reference_construction('a29_working_party', 'edpb')

GDPR_A95.title: Relationship with Directive 2002/58/EC
GDPR_A95.text: This Regulation shall not impose additional obligations on natural or legal persons in relation to processing in connection with the provision of publicly available electronic communications services in public communication networks in the Union in relation to matters for which they are subject to specific obligations with the same objective set out in Directive 2002/58/EC.
GDPR_A95.datalog_rule:
% GDPR_A95 — Relationship with Directive 2002/58/EC (ePrivacy)
