AIAct_A6.1.b.text: the product whose safety component pursuant to point (a) is the AI system, or the AI system itself as a product, is required to undergo a third-party conformity assessment, with a view to the placing on the market or the putting into service of that product pursuant to the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I.
AIAct_A6.1.b.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P1_PT_b — third-party assessment required
high_risk_ai_basis_b(X, Y, L) <= ai_system(X) & safety_component_of(X, Y) & subject_to_third_party_assessment(Y, L)
high_risk_ai_basis_b(X, X, L) <= ai_system(X) & is_product(X) & subject_to_third_party_assessment(X, L)

AIAct_A6.1.text: Irrespective of whether an AI system is placed on the market or put into service independently of the products referred to in points (a) and (b), that AI system shall be considered to be high-risk where both of the following conditions are fulfilled:
AIAct_A6.1.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P1
high_risk_ai(X) <= ai_system(X)  & safety_component_of(X, Y)  & annex_i_legislation(L)  & subject_to_third_party_assessment(Y, L)

% AIAct_A6_P1_alt
high_risk_ai(X) <= ai_system(X)  & annex_i_legislation(L)  & subject_to_third_party_assessment(X, L)

AIAct_A6.2.text: In addition to the high-risk AI systems referred to in paragraph 1, AI systems referred to in Annex III shall be considered to be high-risk.
AIAct_A6.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P2
high_risk_ai(X) <= ai_system(X) & used_in(X, U) & annex_iii_use_case(U)

AIAct_A6.3.a.text: the AI system is intended to perform a narrow procedural task;

AIAct_A6.3.b.text: the AI system is intended to improve the result of a previously completed human activity;
AIAct_A6.3.b.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P3_PT_b — improve previously completed human activity
a6_p3_carveout_condition(X) <= intended_to_improve_previously_completed_human_activity(X)

AIAct_A6.3.c.text: the AI system is intended to detect decision-making patterns or deviations from prior decision-making patterns and is not meant to replace or influence the previously completed human assessment, without proper human review; or
AIAct_A6.3.c.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P3_PT_c — detect decision-making patterns/deviations w/o replacing human review
a6_p3_carveout_condition(X) <= intended_to_detect_decision_patterns(X) & ~replaces_human_assessment_without_review(X)

AIAct_A6.3.d.text:
the AI system is intended to perform a preparatory task to an assessment relevant for the purposes of the use cases listed in Annex III.

Notwithstanding the first subparagraph, an AI system referred to in Annex III shall always be considered to be high-risk where the AI system performs profiling of natural persons.
AIAct_A6.3.d.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P3_PT_d — preparatory task to an Annex III assessment
a6_p3_carveout_condition(X) <= intended_to_perform_preparatory_task(X) & relevant_to_annex_iii_use_case(X)

AIAct_A6.3.text:
By derogation from paragraph 2, an AI system referred to in Annex III shall not be considered to be high-risk where it does not pose a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons, including by not materially influencing the outcome of decision making.

The first subparagraph shall apply where any of the following conditions is fulfilled:
AIAct_A6.3.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P3 — chapeau of derogation: low-risk Annex III carve-out (positive 'not high-risk' label)
high_risk_ai_carveout(X) <= ai_system(X) & annex_iii_use_case(U) & used_in(X, U) & a6_p3_carveout_condition(X) & ~a6_p3_profiling_override(X)
a6_p3_profiling_override(X) <= performs_profiling_of_natural_persons(X)

AIAct_A6.4.text: A provider who considers that an AI system referred to in Annex III is not high-risk shall document its assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into service. Such provider shall be subject to the registration obligation set out in Article 49(2). Upon request of national competent authorities, the provider shall provide the documentation of the assessment.
AIAct_A6.4.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P4 — provider documents non-high-risk assessment & registers
obligation(P, X, 'document_non_high_risk_assessment_before_market') <= provider(P, X) & ai_system(X) & annex_iii_use_case(U) & used_in(X, U) & ~high_risk_ai(X)
obligation(P, X, 'register_under_a49_p2') <= provider(P, X) & ai_system(X) & annex_iii_use_case(U) & used_in(X, U) & ~high_risk_ai(X)
obligation(P, X, 'provide_assessment_documentation_on_request') <= provider(P, X) & ai_system(X) & annex_iii_use_case(U) & used_in(X, U) & ~high_risk_ai(X)

AIAct_A6.5.text: The Commission shall, after consulting the European Artificial Intelligence Board (the ‘Board’), and no later than 2 February 2026, provide guidelines specifying the practical implementation of this Article in line with Article 96 together with a comprehensive list of practical examples of use cases of AI systems that are high-risk and not high-risk.
AIAct_A6.5.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P5 — Commission to publish guidelines by 2026-02-02
+ commission_deadline('ai_act', 'a6_guidelines_high_risk', '2026-02-02')

AIAct_A6.6.text: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 in order to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, of this Article by adding new conditions to those laid down therein, or by modifying them, where there is concrete and reliable evidence of the existence of AI systems that fall under the scope of Annex III, but do not pose a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons.
AIAct_A6.6.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P6 — Commission empowered: add conditions to A6(3)
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a6_p6', 'add_conditions_to_a6_p3')

AIAct_A6.7.text: The Commission shall adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 in order to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, of this Article by deleting any of the conditions laid down therein, where there is concrete and reliable evidence that this is necessary to maintain the level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights provided for by this Regulation.
AIAct_A6.7.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P7 — Commission empowered: delete conditions from A6(3)
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a6_p7', 'delete_conditions_from_a6_p3')

AIAct_A6.8.text: Any amendment to the conditions laid down in paragraph 3, second subparagraph, adopted in accordance with paragraphs 6 and 7 of this Article shall not decrease the overall level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights provided for by this Regulation and shall ensure consistency with the delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 7(1), and take account of market and technological developments.
AIAct_A6.8.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A6_P8 — amendments must not decrease overall protection
constraint_on_amendment('ai_act', 'a6_p3', 'no_decrease_in_overall_protection')

AIAct_A7.title: Amendments to Annex III
AIAct_A7.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7 — Amendments to Annex III (chapeau)
ai_act_scope_article('ai_act_a7', 'amendments_to_annex_iii')

AIAct_A7.1.a.text: the AI systems are intended to be used in any of the areas listed in Annex III;
AIAct_A7.1.a.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P1_PT_a — system in any Annex III area
a7_p1_condition('a7_p1_a', 'within_annex_iii_area')

AIAct_A7.1.b.text:
the AI systems pose a risk of harm to health and safety, or an adverse impact on fundamental rights, and that risk is equivalent to, or greater than, the risk of harm or of adverse impact posed by the high-risk AI systems already referred to in Annex III.

54/144                                                                                   ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj
AIAct_A7.1.b.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P1_PT_b — system poses equivalent or greater risk
a7_p1_condition('a7_p1_b', 'risk_equivalent_or_greater_than_existing_annex_iii')

AIAct_A7.1.text: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend Annex III by adding or modifying use-cases of high-risk AI systems where both of the following conditions are fulfilled:
AIAct_A7.1.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P1 — Commission empowered to amend Annex III when both conditions met
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a7_p1', 'add_or_modify_annex_iii_use_cases')

AIAct_A7.2.a.text: the intended purpose of the AI system;

AIAct_A7.2.b.text: the extent to which an AI system has been used or is likely to be used;

AIAct_A7.2.c.text: the nature and amount of the data processed and used by the AI system, in particular whether special categories of personal data are processed;
AIAct_A7.2.c.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P2_PT_c — nature/amount of data; special categories
a7_p2_criterion('data_nature_special_categories')

AIAct_A7.2.d.text: the extent to which the AI system acts autonomously and the possibility for a human to override a decision or recommendations that may lead to potential harm;

AIAct_A7.2.e.text: the extent to which the use of an AI system has already caused harm to health and safety, has had an adverse impact on fundamental rights or has given rise to significant concerns in relation to the likelihood of such harm or adverse impact, as demonstrated, for example, by reports or documented allegations submitted to national competent authorities or by other reports, as appropriate;

AIAct_A7.2.f.text: the potential extent of such harm or such adverse impact, in particular in terms of its intensity and its ability to affect multiple persons or to disproportionately affect a particular group of persons;

AIAct_A7.2.g.text: the extent to which persons who are potentially harmed or suffer an adverse impact are dependent on the outcome produced with an AI system, in particular because for practical or legal reasons it is not reasonably possible to opt-out from that outcome;

AIAct_A7.2.h.i.text: the extent to which the outcome produced involving an AI system is easily corrigible or reversible, taking into account the technical solutions available to correct or reverse it, whereby outcomes having an adverse impact on health, safety or fundamental rights, shall not be considered to be easily corrigible or reversible;

AIAct_A7.2.h.text: the extent to which there is an imbalance of power, or the persons who are potentially harmed or suffer an adverse impact are in a vulnerable position in relation to the deployer of an AI system, in particular due to status, authority, knowledge, economic or social circumstances, or age;

AIAct_A7.2.j.text: the magnitude and likelihood of benefit of the deployment of the AI system for individuals, groups, or society at large, including possible improvements in product safety;

AIAct_A7.2.k.i.text: effective measures of redress in relation to the risks posed by an AI system, with the exclusion of claims for damages;
AIAct_A7.2.k.i.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P2_PT_k_i — effective measures of redress
a7_p2_criterion_subkind('a7_p2_k', 'effective_redress_measures')

AIAct_A7.2.k.ii.text: effective measures to prevent or substantially minimise those risks.
AIAct_A7.2.k.ii.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P2_PT_k_ii — effective measures to prevent/minimise risk
a7_p2_criterion_subkind('a7_p2_k', 'effective_risk_prevention_measures')

AIAct_A7.2.k.text: the extent to which existing Union law provides for:

AIAct_A7.2.text: When assessing the condition under paragraph 1, point (b), the Commission shall take into account the following criteria:
AIAct_A7.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P2 — chapeau: criteria for assessing A7(1)(b)
a7_p2_criterion_kind('intended_purpose')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('extent_of_use')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('data_nature_amount_special_categories')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('autonomy_human_override')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('past_harm_evidence')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('extent_of_potential_harm')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('dependence_on_outcome')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('imbalance_of_power_vulnerability')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('benefit_magnitude_likelihood')
a7_p2_criterion_kind('existing_redress_and_prevention')

AIAct_A7.3.a.text: the high-risk AI system concerned no longer poses any significant risks to fundamental rights, health or safety, taking into account the criteria listed in paragraph 2;

AIAct_A7.3.b.text:
the deletion does not decrease the overall level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights under Union law.

SECTION 2 Requirements for high-risk AI systems
AIAct_A7.3.b.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P3_PT_b — deletion does not decrease overall protection
a7_p3_condition('a7_p3_b', 'no_decrease_in_overall_protection')

AIAct_A7.3.text: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend the list in Annex III by removing high-risk AI systems where both of the following conditions are fulfilled:
AIAct_A7.3.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A7_P3 — Commission empowered to remove from Annex III when both met
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a7_p3', 'remove_from_annex_iii')

AIAct_A50.title: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems

AIAct_A50.1.text: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence.
AIAct_A50.1.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P1 — providers: design AI to inform natural persons of AI interaction
obligation(P, X, 'inform_persons_of_ai_interaction') <= provider(P, X) & ai_system(X) & intended_for_direct_interaction(X) & ~obvious_to_reasonable_person(X) & ~le_authorised_with_safeguards(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= ai_system(X) & intended_for_direct_interaction(X)

AIAct_A50.2.text: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated. Providers shall ensure their technical solutions are effective, interoperable, robust and reliable as far as this is technically feasible, taking into account the specificities and limitations of various types of content, the costs of implementation and the generally acknowledged state of the art, as may be reflected in relevant technical standards. This obligation shall not apply to the extent the AI systems perform an assistive function for standard editing or do not substantially alter the input data provided by the deployer or the semantics thereof, or where authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences.
AIAct_A50.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P2 — providers of synthetic-content AI: machine-readable marking
obligation(P, X, 'mark_synthetic_outputs_machine_readable') <= provider(P, X) & ai_system(X) & generates_synthetic_content(X) & ~assistive_editing_only(X) & ~le_authorised_with_safeguards(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= ai_system(X) & generates_synthetic_content(X)

AIAct_A50.3.text: Deployers of an emotion recognition system or a biometric categorisation system shall inform the natural persons exposed thereto of the operation of the system, and shall process the personal data in accordance with Regulations (EU) 2016/679 and (EU) 2018/1725 and Directive (EU) 2016/680, as applicable. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems used for biometric categorisation and emotion recognition, which are permitted by law to detect, prevent or investigate criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, and in accordance with Union law.
AIAct_A50.3.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P3 — deployers of emotion/biometric-categorisation: inform persons & comply with DP law
obligation(D, X, 'inform_natural_persons_of_emotion_or_categorisation') <= deployer(D, X) & emotion_recognition_system(X) & ~le_permitted_with_safeguards(X)
obligation(D, X, 'inform_natural_persons_of_emotion_or_categorisation') <= deployer(D, X) & biometric_categorisation_system(X) & ~le_permitted_with_safeguards(X)
obligation(D, X, 'comply_with_dp_law') <= deployer(D, X) & emotion_recognition_system(X)
obligation(D, X, 'comply_with_dp_law') <= deployer(D, X) & biometric_categorisation_system(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= emotion_recognition_system(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= biometric_categorisation_system(X)

AIAct_A50.4.text:
Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated. This obligation shall not apply where the use is authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offence. Where the content forms part of an evidently artistic, creative, satirical, fictional or analogous work or programme, the transparency obligations set out in this paragraph are limited to disclosure of the existence of such generated or manipulated content in an appropriate manner that does not hamper the display or enjoyment of the work.

Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text which is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest shall disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated. This obligation shall not apply where the use is authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences or where the AI-generated content has undergone a process of human review or editorial control and where a natural or legal person holds editorial responsibility for the publication of the content.

82/144                                                                                       ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj
AIAct_A50.4.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P4 — deployers of deep-fake / public-interest AI text: disclosure
obligation(D, X, 'disclose_deep_fake_content') <= deployer(D, X) & ai_system(X) & generates_deep_fake_output(X) & ~le_authorised(X)
obligation(D, X, 'disclose_ai_text_for_public_interest_news') <= deployer(D, X) & ai_system(X) & generates_text_for_public_interest_news(X) & ~human_editorial_review(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= ai_system(X) & generates_deep_fake_output(X)
transparency_obligation_applies(X) <= ai_system(X) & generates_text_for_public_interest_news(X)

AIAct_A50.5.text: The information referred to in paragraphs 1 to 4 shall be provided to the natural persons concerned in a clear and distinguishable manner at the latest at the time of the first interaction or exposure. The information shall conform to the applicable accessibility requirements.
AIAct_A50.5.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P5 — information must be clear, distinguishable, accessible, at first interaction
obligation(A, X, 'inform_at_first_interaction_clearly_and_accessibly') <= obligation(A, X, OBLG) & a50_transparency_obligation(OBLG)
a50_transparency_obligation('inform_persons_of_ai_interaction')
a50_transparency_obligation('mark_synthetic_outputs_machine_readable')
a50_transparency_obligation('inform_natural_persons_of_emotion_or_categorisation')
a50_transparency_obligation('disclose_deep_fake_content')
a50_transparency_obligation('disclose_ai_text_for_public_interest_news')

AIAct_A50.6.text: Paragraphs 1 to 4 shall not affect the requirements and obligations set out in Chapter III, and shall be without prejudice to other transparency obligations laid down in Union or national law for deployers of AI systems.
AIAct_A50.6.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P6 — without prejudice to Chapter III & other transparency law
ai_act_without_prejudice_to('chapter_iii_high_risk_obligations')
ai_act_without_prejudice_to('other_union_or_national_transparency_law')

AIAct_A50.7.text:
The AI Office shall encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of practice at Union level to facilitate the effective implementation of the obligations regarding the detection and labelling of artificially generated or manipulated content. The Commission may adopt implementing acts to approve those codes of practice in accordance with the procedure laid down in Article 56 (6). If it deems the code is not adequate, the Commission may adopt an implementing act specifying common rules for the implementation of those obligations in accordance with the examination procedure laid down in Article 98(2).

CHAPTER V GENERAL-PURPOSE AI MODELS

SECTION 1 Classification rules
AIAct_A50.7.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A50_P7 — AI Office to facilitate codes of practice on labelling
obligation(AO, _, 'facilitate_codes_of_practice_for_labelling') <= ai_office(AO)
commission_implementing_act_basis('ai_act', 'a50_p7', 'approve_or_specify_labelling_rules')

AIAct_A51.title: Classification of general-purpose AI models as general-purpose AI models with systemic risk
AIAct_A51.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A51 — Classification of GPAI as systemic-risk (chapeau)
ai_act_scope_article('ai_act_a51', 'gpai_systemic_risk_classification')

AIAct_A51.1.a.text: it has high impact capabilities evaluated on the basis of appropriate technical tools and methodologies, including indicators and benchmarks;

AIAct_A51.1.b.text: based on a decision of the Commission, ex officio or following a qualified alert from the scientific panel, it has capabilities or an impact equivalent to those set out in point (a) having regard to the criteria set out in Annex XIII.

AIAct_A51.1.text: A general-purpose AI model shall be classified as a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk if it meets any of the following conditions:
AIAct_A51.1.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A51_P1 — chapeau (rules carried by points)
gpai_systemic_risk(X) <= gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'compute_above_10e25')
gpai_systemic_risk(X) <= gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'commission_designated')

AIAct_A51.2.text: A general-purpose AI model shall be presumed to have high impact capabilities pursuant to paragraph 1, point (a), when the cumulative amount of computation used for its training measured in floating point operations is greater than 1025.
AIAct_A51.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A51_P2 — presumption: compute > 10^25 FLOPs => high-impact capabilities
has_feature(X, 'compute_above_10e25') <= gpai_model(X) & training_compute_flops(X, F) & f_greater_than_10e25(F)

AIAct_A51.3.text: The Commission shall adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend the thresholds listed in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article, as well as to supplement benchmarks and indicators in light of evolving technological developments, such as algorithmic improvements or increased hardware efficiency, when necessary, for these thresholds to reflect the state of the art.
AIAct_A51.3.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A51_P3 — Commission empowered to amend thresholds
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a51_p3', 'amend_a51_thresholds_and_benchmarks')

AIAct_A52.1.text: Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met. That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.
AIAct_A52.1.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P1 — provider notifies Commission within 2 weeks
obligation(P, X, 'notify_commission_within_2_weeks') <= provider(P, X) & gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'compute_above_10e25')
% Commission may designate sua sponte
commission_power('ai_act', 'a52_p1', 'designate_systemic_risk_sua_sponte')

AIAct_A52.2.text: The provider of a general-purpose AI model that meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), may present, with its notification, sufficiently substantiated arguments to demonstrate that, exceptionally, although it meets that requirement, the general-purpose AI model does not present, due to its specific characteristics, systemic risks and therefore should not be classified as a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk.
AIAct_A52.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P2 — provider may rebut presumption with substantiated arguments
provider_right(P, X, 'rebut_systemic_risk_presumption') <= provider(P, X) & gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'compute_above_10e25')

AIAct_A52.3.text: Where the Commission concludes that the arguments submitted pursuant to paragraph 2 are not sufficiently substantiated and the relevant provider was not able to demonstrate that the general-purpose AI model does not present, due to its specific characteristics, systemic risks, it shall reject those arguments, and the general-purpose AI model shall be considered to be a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk.
AIAct_A52.3.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P3 — Commission may reject rebuttal -> systemic-risk classification confirmed
gpai_systemic_risk(X) <= gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'compute_above_10e25') & rebuttal_rejected_by_commission(X)

AIAct_A52.4.text:
The Commission may designate a general-purpose AI model as presenting systemic risks, ex officio or following a qualified alert from the scientific panel pursuant to Article 90(1), point (a), on the basis of criteria set out in Annex XIII.

The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 in order to amend Annex XIII by specifying and updating the criteria set out in that Annex.
AIAct_A52.4.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P4 — Commission may designate sua sponte using Annex XIII
gpai_systemic_risk(X) <= gpai_model(X) & has_feature(X, 'commission_designated')
commission_delegated_act_basis('ai_act', 'a52_p4', 'amend_annex_xiii_criteria')

AIAct_A52.5.text: Upon a reasoned request of a provider whose model has been designated as a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk pursuant to paragraph 4, the Commission shall take the request into account and may decide to reassess whether the general-purpose AI model can still be considered to present systemic risks on the basis of the criteria set out in Annex XIII. Such a request shall contain objective, detailed and new reasons that have arisen since the designation decision. Providers may request reassessment at the earliest six months after the designation decision. Where the Commission, following its reassessment, decides to maintain the designation as a general-purpose AI model with systemic risk, providers may request reassessment at the earliest six months after that decision.
AIAct_A52.5.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P5 — provider may request reassessment after 6 months
provider_right(P, X, 'request_reassessment_after_6_months') <= provider(P, X) & gpai_systemic_risk(X)

AIAct_A52.6.text:
The Commission shall ensure that a list of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk is published and shall keep that list up to date, without prejudice to the need to observe and protect intellectual property rights and confidential business information or trade secrets in accordance with Union and national law.

SECTION 2 Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
AIAct_A52.6.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A52_P6 — Commission publishes & maintains list
obligation('commission', _, 'publish_gpai_systemic_risk_list')

AIAct_A111.text: AI systems already placed on the market or put into service and general-purpose AI models already placed on the marked
AIAct_A111.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A111 — Pre-deployed systems: transitional grace (chapeau)
ai_act_scope_article('ai_act_a111', 'pre_deployed_transitional')

AIAct_A111.1.text:
Without prejudice to the application of Article 5 as referred to in Article 113(3), point (a), AI systems which are components of the large-scale IT systems established by the legal acts listed in Annex X that have been placed on the market or put into service before 2 August 2027 shall be brought into compliance with this Regulation by 31 December 2030.

The requirements laid down in this Regulation shall be taken into account in the evaluation of each large-scale IT system established by the legal acts listed in Annex X to be undertaken as provided for in those legal acts and where those legal acts are replaced or amended.

AIAct_A111.2.text: Without prejudice to the application of Article 5 as referred to in Article 113(3), point (a), this Regulation shall apply to operators of high-risk AI systems, other than the systems referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, that have been placed on the market or put into service before 2 August 2026, only if, as from that date, those systems are subject to significant changes in their designs. In any case, the providers and deployers of high-risk AI systems intended to be used by public authorities shall take the necessary steps to comply with the requirements and obligations of this Regulation by 2 August 2030.
AIAct_A111.2.datalog_rule:
% AIAct_A111_P2 — high-risk pre-deployed (other than A111(1)): apply only if significant changes after 2026-08-02
ai_act_applies_pre_deployed(S) <= high_risk_ai(S) & placed_on_market_before(S, '2026-08-02') & significant_changes_after(S, '2026-08-02')

% public-authority deployers: full compliance by 2030-08-02
+ application_date('ai_act', 'high_risk_predeployed_public_authority', '2030-08-02')

AIAct_A111.3.text:
Providers of general-purpose AI models that have been placed on the market before 2 August 2025 shall take the necessary steps in order to comply with the obligations laid down in this Regulation by 2 August 2027.

protection of the collective interests of consumers and repealing Directive 2009/22/EC (OJ L 409, 4.12.2020, p. 1).

AIAct_A113.title: Entry into force and application
AIAct_A113.text: This Regulation shall enter into force on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union. It shall apply from 2 August 2026. However: (a) Chapters I and II shall apply from 2 February 2025; (b) Chapter III Section 4, Chapter V, Chapter VII and Chapter XII and Article 78 shall apply from 2 August 2025, with the exception of Article 101; (c) Article 6(1) and the corresponding obligations in this Regulation shall apply from 2 August 2027.
